epics.title: Remove the Quiz and Chords website surfaces

tasks.title: Remove the Quiz and Chords App Router routes
acceptance_criteria: The Next.js App Router no longer contains page implementations for /quiz or /chords.
Route-local components used only by the removed pages are removed.
Unrelated music utilities and website pages remain unchanged.
The Builder follows the repository design-rule-engine and vercel:nextjs route conventions.
done_definition: src/app/quiz and src/app/chords no longer exist as route segments.

tasks.title: Remove navigation entry points to the deleted routes
acceptance_criteria: The shared primary navigation no longer includes Quiz or Chords links.
The remaining navigation items retain their order and active-state behavior.
No runtime source file links to /quiz or /chords.
done_definition: Runtime navigation exposes only the remaining supported routes.

tasks.title: Verify route and navigation removal
acceptance_criteria: A source search finds no runtime links or route files for /quiz or /chords.
The production route manifest omits /quiz and /chords.
The rendered shared navigation omits Quiz and Chords while a representative remaining page loads successfully.
Lint, production build, and AI run validation pass.
done_definition: verify.json records deterministic and browser-backed evidence with no blocking issues.

assumptions: The user's phrase 'quiz chords 페이지' refers to the two existing standalone routes /quiz and /chords.
Historical documentation and prior AI run artifacts should remain as project history.
Shared music helpers that may be used by other routes should not be removed solely because a deleted page imported them.
risks: An undocumented external bookmark may still target a removed route and will receive the application's normal not-found response.
Deleting route-local components requires confirming that no remaining runtime source imports them.
